perturbation.original_text: 2. Definition. "Confidential Information" means any information, technical data, or know - how, inc luding, but not limited to, that which relates to research, product plans, products, services, customers, markets, opt -in information including Short -code and URL information, marketing or finances, which Confidential Information is designated in writing t o be confidential or proprietary, or if given orally, is confirmed promptly in writing as having been disclosed as confidential or proprietary.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Definition. "Confidential Information" means any information, technical data, or know - how, inc luding, but not limited to, that which relates to research, product plans, products, services, customers, markets, opt -in information including Short -code and URL information, marketing or finances, which Confidential Information is designated in writing to be confidential or proprietary, or if given orally, must also be confirmed by a senior executive of the disclosing party in writing within 5 business days as having been disclosed as confidential or proprietary.
perturbation.explanation: The change adds a requirement for senior executive confirmation within 5 days for orally disclosed confidential information. This contradicts the original definition, which only required 'prompt' written confirmation, creating ambiguity. If section 3 mentions the original definition, there will be an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non -Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that s et forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. The Recipient will not disclose any Confidential Information to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient who are required to have the information in order to car ry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. Recipient has had or will have directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient to whom Confidential Information is disclosed or who have access to Confidential Information sig n a Non -Disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this Agreement and will promptly notify the Discloser in writing of the names of each such person who has signed such agreements after such agreements are signed. Recipient agrees that it wil l take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have a ny such information, which measures shall include the highest degree of care that Recipient utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. Recipient agrees to notify the Discloser in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of s uch Confidential Information which may come to its attention. Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA )
perturbation.justification: The change to the definition of 'Confidential Information' in Section 2 adds a requirement for senior executive confirmation within 5 days for orally disclosed confidential information. This contradicts the original definition, which only required 'prompt' written confirmation. Section 3 discusses the non-disclosure of confidential information but relies on the general definition. The modified definition introduces a new constraint (senior executive confirmation), which may not be explicitly addressed or accounted for in Section 3, creating a potential contradiction because prompt notification is no longer enough, and if the senior executive doesn't confirm within 5 days, it's not confidential, and Section 3 no longer applies. This would lead to significant ambiguity. The prompt notification timeframe also changed.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Non -Disclosure of Confidential Information. Recipient agrees that it wil l take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have a ny such information, which measures shall include the highest degree of care that Recipient utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. Recipient agrees to notify the Discloser in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of s uch Confidential Information which may come to its attention. Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA )
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Non -Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that s et forth in Section 1 of this Agreement, unless explicitly authorized in writing by the Discloser. The Recipient will not disclose any Confidential Information to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient who are required to have the information in order to car ry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. Recipient has had or will have directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient to whom Confidential Information is disclosed or who have access to Confidential Information sig n a Non -Disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this Agreement and will promptly notify the Discloser in writing of the names of each such person who has signed such agreements after such agreements are signed. Recipient agrees that it wil l take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have a ny such information, which measures shall include the highest degree of care that Recipient utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. Recipient agrees to notify the Discloser in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of s uch Confidential Information which may come to its attention. Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA )
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'unless explicitly authorized in writing by the Discloser,' the scope of permissible use of Confidential Information widens beyond Section 1, contradicting the initial restriction. This creates an in-text contradiction in section 1, because it defines the purpose of the agreement, and section 3 now makes an exception to that purpose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Purpose. The Discloser has agreed to make available to the Recipient certain Confidential Information (as defined below) of the Discloser for the purpose of evaluating a possible business transaction with the Discloser.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 3 restricts the use of Confidential Information to the purpose set forth in Section 1, which is 'evaluating a possible business transaction.' The changed text adds 'unless explicitly authorized in writing by the Discloser.' This broadens the permissible use beyond the original purpose defined in Section 1. Therefore, Section 3 now contradicts Section 1, as the Recipient can use the information for other purposes if authorized in writing, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction because it allows a different purpose of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Return of Materials. Any materials or documents of which have been furnished by the Discloser to the Recipient will be promptly returned, accompanied by copies of such doc umentation, after the evaluation set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement has been concluded.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Return of Materials. Any materials or documents of which have been furnished by the Discloser to the Recipient will be returned within 30 days of a written request from the Discloser, but the Recipient may retain one archival copy of all documentation.
perturbation.explanation: The change alters the return conditions from 'promptly returned after evaluation' to 'within 30 days of written request' and allows retention of an archival copy. This directly contradicts the original, making the return timeline and obligations ambiguous. Contradicts section 1, because the evaluation might not be concluded at the time of returning the materials.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Purpose. The Discloser has agreed to make available to the Recipient certain Confidential Information (as defined below) of the Discloser for the purpose of evaluating a possible business transaction with the Discloser.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5 alters the original return conditions from 'promptly returned after evaluation' to 'within 30 days of written request' and allows retention of an archival copy. This directly contradicts the original, making the return timeline and obligations ambiguous. As the Perturbation Explanation states, this contradicts Section 1, which states that the recipient is evaluating a possible business transaction with the Discloser. The evaluation might not be concluded at the time of returning the materials, because the recipient can hold onto it for 30 days, creating a strong contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: 6. No License Granted. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to Recipient under any patent, copyright, trade secret or other intellectual prop erty right nor shall this Agreement grant Recipient any rights in or to the other party's Confidential Information, except the limited right to review such Confidential Information solely for the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. No License Granted. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to Recipient under any patent, copyright, trade secret or other intellectual prop erty right nor shall this Agreement grant Recipient any rights in or to the other party's Confidential Information, except the right to use anonymized and aggregated Confidential Information for internal research purposes, provided such use does not reveal the Discloser's identity.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces an exception, allowing the Recipient to use anonymized and aggregated Confidential Information for internal research. This is a new right and goes beyond the 'limited right to review' specified in the original text, introducing a conflicting statement. Therefore there will be a contradiction in section 1, due to the limited use of the information
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Purpose. The Discloser has agreed to make available to the Recipient certain Confidential Information (as defined below) of the Discloser for the purpose of evaluating a possible business transaction with the Discloser.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 6 granted only a 'limited right to review' Confidential Information for a specific purpose (Section 1). The changed text expands this right to include 'use of anonymized and aggregated Confidential Information for internal research purposes.' This directly contradicts the limited purpose stated in Section 1, which focuses on evaluating a 'possible business transaction.' The new permitted use is broader than the initial purpose, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Term. T he foregoing commitments shall survive any termination of discussions between the parties, and shall continue for a period of three (3) years following the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Term. T he foregoing commitments shall survive any termination of discussions between the parties, and shall continue for a period of three (3) years following the date of this Agreement, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties', the original definite term of 3 years is made conditional. This introduces ambiguity as the commitment period is no longer fixed, and hence there is an in-text contradiction with section 10, which requires recipent to take appropriate action.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Re medies. Recipient agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Discloser and its business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate the Discloser for any breach of any covena nts and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, Recipient agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation will cause irreparable injury to the Discloser and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, the Discloser shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages. For and on behalf of For and on be half of MMGRP Limited [ ] Print Name Print Name Position Position Date: Date: MMGRP Limited , 145 – 157 St Johns Stre et, London , EC1V 4PW Company Number: 07527443 DPA Registration Number: Z2553231 t. +44 (0) 207 873 2421 f. +44 (0) 844 443 2349 e. sales@mmgrp.co.uk
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a conditional term ('unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties') to the previously definite three-year commitment outlined in Section 7. This directly contradicts Section 10, which relies on a firm commitment from the Recipient to protect the Discloser's business and assumes monetary damages would be inadequate for any breach. The added conditionality weakens this commitment and creates ambiguity about the duration of the Recipient's obligations, thus creating an in-text contradiction as per the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Re medies. Recipient agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Discloser and its business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate the Discloser for any breach of any covena nts and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, Recipient agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation will cause irreparable injury to the Discloser and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, the Discloser shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Re medies. Recipient agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Discloser and its business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate the Discloser for any breach of any covena nts and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, Recipient agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation will cause irreparable injury to the Discloser and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, the Discloser shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages. However, the Recipient's maximum liability under this agreement shall not exceed £1000.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'However, the Recipient's maximum liability under this agreement shall not exceed £1000' introduces a cap on liability, which directly contradicts the previous statement that monetary damages would be inadequate and injunctive relief is available without proving actual damages. There is an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Re medies. Recipient agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Discloser and its business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate the Discloser for any breach of any covena nts and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, Recipient agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation will cause irreparable injury to the Discloser and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, the Discloser shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages. However, the Recipient's maximum liability under this agreement shall not exceed £1000.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a cap on the recipient's liability (£1000), which directly contradicts the earlier statement in the same section (and repeated in the contradicted text) that monetary damages would be inadequate and that the discloser is entitled to injunctive relief without proving actual damages. This creates a clear and direct conflict, making enforcement unclear as the cap limits the available remedies, thereby aligning with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'. The added clause means that in theory, the Recipient could breach the agreement so long as the damages don't exceed £1000. The contradicted text and the changed text are in the same section, and the change introduces a clause that directly contradicts the original clause.
